title: Jupyter
description: Jupyter embeds a full notebook workbench inside Parthenon for exploratory analysis, custom figures, cohort diagnostics, and study-specific research workflows. The embedded lab mounts a persistent notebook workspace backed by output/jupyter-notebook and provides a starter Parthenon notebook so researchers can move from cohort and study workflows into code without leaving the platform.
tips: Use the embedded lab for interactive exploration, then open the same workspace in a new tab when you need more screen space.
The notebook workspace persists in output/jupyter-notebook, so anything you save in Jupyter stays in the repository and can be versioned.
Start with the seeded Parthenon Research Workbench notebook to verify the environment and pick up the recommended workflow.
Use Jupyter for custom subgroup checks, sensitivity analyses, GIS joins, and one-off charts that do not justify a dedicated product feature.
Keep notebooks study-oriented: name files clearly and attach resulting artifacts back to the relevant study or publication workflow.
The container exposes Parthenon API and database connection hints via environment variables for advanced scripted workflows.
